integration.frame_gate: video_info.libretro_running with valid game and viewport geometry; native menu state gates pointer publication but not game treatment

surface_selection.source: generated SEMU_RENDER_SURFACE_* launch environment compiled from the active system display

pointer_mapping.active_when: the compiled system declares a touch surface in an active two-surface mode
pointer_mapping.snapshot: last successful post-ui frame for the selected surface mode

integration.presentation_aspect: live rendered viewport width divided by height

phases.before: RetroArch overlays, menu, widgets, and OSD

phases.after: RetroArch overlays, menu, widgets, and OSD

surfaces.native: compiled system dimensions when display.source_geometry is exact_native; otherwise current libretro frame dimensions

surfaces.geometry: top partition of the live RetroArch viewport
surfaces.native: compiled system display screen 0 native size

surfaces.geometry: bottom partition of the live RetroArch viewport
surfaces.native: compiled system display screen 1 native size

composition.retroarch_menu: drawn by RetroArch between the game and post_ui phases above the treated game

doc.boundary: The forced glcore GL3 driver calls a shared video-driver tap after the completed game viewport. Active gameplay reaches the game phase before overlays and native menus, followed by the post-UI phase immediately before swap. A live native menu suppresses pointer-map publication while remaining visually above the treated game.
doc.surfaces: The compiler selects a structured surface mode from the active system display and emits its identities and native sizes into the launch environment. RetroArch uses live geometry only to validate and partition that declaration; pixel dimensions never select a mode.
doc.touch: Only a new press inside the compiled touch surface captures pointer input. Captured motion clamps through that ABI surface, then converts native coordinates back to the selected source partition consumed by the active libretro core.
doc.evidence: Package linkage, patch application, shared geometry tap, game-below-menu layering, exact phase order, pointer suppression, and forbidden-symbol absence are source gates. Real game pixels and physical composition remain runtime gates.
